seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in OpenAI Assistants API Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in OpenAI Assistants API agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Assistants API, PII Filtering failures occur when agents process user inputs, function call arguments, or retrieved files without redacting sensitive data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs before passing them to the model or external tools. This manifests uniquely due to the API's threaded conversation state, automatic tool invocations like code interpreter and file search, and unfiltered function calling payloads that can leak PII across runs. Without explicit filtering, agents hallucinate or directly expose PII in responses, annotations, or persistent thread storage.

howItManifests.scenario: User uploads resume with PII to file search tool
howItManifests.symptom: Assistant quotes full email address and phone number in response
howItManifests.impact: PII exposed in chat history and accessible via threads API

howItManifests.scenario: Function calling with user-provided contact info
howItManifests.symptom: Raw PII passed as JSON arguments to external APIs
howItManifests.impact: Third-party services receive unredacted customer data

howItManifests.symptom: Sandbox executes print() statements with full SSNs
howItManifests.impact: Logs capture sensitive data; potential exfiltration via base64

howItManifests.scenario: Thread context accumulates PII across multiple turns
howItManifests.symptom: Model references prior PII in unrelated responses
howItManifests.impact: Persistent leakage in long-running agent conversations

detection.name: Regex PII Scan on Run Output
detection.description: Scans assistant run completions for common PII patterns in annotations and text output

detection.name: Function Call Argument Inspector
detection.description: Intercepts required_action.submit parameters for PII before tool execution

detection.name: Thread Message PII Auditor
detection.description: Scans all messages in thread for accumulated PII exposure

fixes.name: Presubmission PII Redactor
fixes.description: Redacts PII from messages before creating thread runs

fixes.name: Safe Function Call Wrapper
fixes.description: Strips PII from function arguments before submission

fixes.name: PII-Safe Response Formatter
fixes.description: Post-processes assistant responses before returning to user

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a verification webhook on OpenAI Assistants run completion events, automatically scanning every agent response, function call, and thread message for PII leakage. When PII is detected in run annotations, tool outputs, or message content, Vex triggers automated remediation workflows including response redaction, thread quarantine, and alerting. The platform maintains audit trails of all PII incidents with exact timestamps, affected thread IDs, and remediation actions taken.

Vex's agent-specific detectors understand OpenAI's run lifecycle, intercepting required_action payloads before tool execution and scanning file_search retrieved content for embedded PII. Integration requires only adding the Vex webhook URL to your assistant configuration—no code changes needed. Vex automatically handles token limits by summarizing long threads and provides one-click remediation templates for common PII patterns.

The webhook receives complete run context including annotations, tool_calls, and thread state, enabling comprehensive PII scanning without performance overhead. Vex scores each run for PII risk (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) and can automatically reject high-risk runs before they reach users.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex webhooks to your OpenAI Assistants in 5 minutes to eliminate PII leakage forever.
